history: The client has recently moved and is experiencing stress from settling into a new living space. They have a difficult relationship with their parents, especially their mother, who is described as judgmental and emotionally unsupportive. Friendships have become strained, contributing to their isolation. The client finds solace in intellectual engagement, particularly in mathematics, which serves as a refuge from their strained relationships.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: If I don't excel in mathematics, then I am not valuable. If I rely on others, I will be judged and unsupported.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't handle things on my own, then I am a failure and unworthy of support.
coping_strategies: The client immerses themselves in intellectual activities, particularly mathematics, to avoid the emotional complexities of their personal relationships. They avoid contact with friends and family, particularly their mother, and find comfort in the structured world of mathematics.
situation: Feeling exhausted after attempting to unpack without support from friends.
auto_thought: I can't rely on anyone; I'm all alone in this.
behavior: The client avoids reaching out to friends or family for help and instead focuses on their academic pursuits to distract themselves from their feelings of isolation.
